message:
Fix BrowserReadableStream double-copy, happy_eyeballs test runtime, and loss recovery epoch

browser_stream.rs: Eliminate intermediate allocation in the
rate-limited read path by slicing directly into the caller's ReadBuf
unfilled region instead of allocating a temporary vec and copying
through put_slice. Uses buf.advance() for proper ReadBuf bookkeeping.

happy_eyeballs.rs: Replace futures_lite::future::block_on with the
crate's own runtime Builder in the dual-stack fallback test, ensuring
the async connect runs on the project's executor rather than a bare
block_on that may lack required runtime context.

transport.rs: Fix RFC 9002 congestion recovery epoch tracking —
on_loss_congestion now receives the current timestamp (now_micros)
and uses it for congestion_recovery_start_time instead of the lost
packet's sent time. This correctly marks the start of the recovery
epoch at detection time, not send time, preventing premature cwnd
reductions for packets sent before the epoch but detected after.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
